Hazard marker is labelled.
Gameplay instructions are visible and readable.
Track purpose is readable to a first-time player.
LEFT/RIGHT control meaning is readable to a first-time player.
First-run gameplay screen explains goal, controls, threat, and player object.
Human-readable gameplay contract is verified without runtime JSON.
Gameplay has content depth beyond basic left/right avoidance.
Gameplay visual objects move after Start.
Enemy hazards visibly advance toward the player with readable pressure.
Left/right steering visibly changes player position.
Human input changes gameplay agency, not only UI state.
Player input changes the gameplay outcome/risk path.
Early gameplay cannot immediately fail before readable player response time.
Hazard pacing has a readable delay before threats begin.
First 10 seconds of play are readable and interactive.
Enemy/hazard patterns visibly create pressure toward the player.
Player skill produces reward or risk/outcome change.
Human playtest checklist is represented in runtime evidence.
Target frame rate is at least 60.
Runtime probe reports zero exceptions.
Runtime probe must expose primary object state, scoring reasons, collision reasons, and object counters.
Gameplay runtime evidence must explain why the latest score delta happened.
Gameplay runtime evidence must explain why the latest pulse/boost delta happened.
Runtime probe must expose a valid collision/failure reason schema even before failure.
Runtime probe must expose non-negative object counters for pickups, hazards, boosts, and combo events.
Runtime probe may only claim gameplay depth, motion, enemy pressure, skill/reward, or human agency after capturing a running gameplay state with score and elapsed play time.
A menu-state probe cannot be used as evidence that actual gameplay is playable.

Simulator app binary exists and is arm64.
Simulator appinfo is available for installed app.
Simulator launch command ran and returned launch information.
Initial simulator screenshot exists and is nonblank.
Simulator playthrough video exists and is large enough to contain real footage.
QA must run at least 3 complete gameplay cycles, not only the first 10 seconds or a single attempt.
QA video must cover the repeated playthrough budget derived from the game's single-run length.
Every QA playthrough run must include menu, gameplay, game-over, and retry/after-retry state evidence.
Playthrough interaction script log exists.
checks.actual:
QA playthrough script:
1. write qa_playthrough.flag into simulator app data container
2. launch app with -qaPlaythrough fallback arg
3. record at least 3 complete playthrough cycles
4. each cycle duration budget is 20.0s based on design/runtime evidence
5. each cycle captures menu, gameplay, game-over, after-retry, and after-retry-live evidence
6. QA cannot pass if only the first 10 seconds or a single attempt is recorded
